title: 10 best herbal capsules for period cramps
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for period cramps are a natural supplement designed to alleviate menstrual discomfort and pain. These capsules contain herbal extracts such as chamomile, ginger, and turmeric, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from cramping. For example, capsules containing chasteberry have been shown to decrease the severity of PMS symptoms, including cramps, while capsules with Dong Quai help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to crampiness. By taking these capsules, women can experience significant reduction in period pain, allowing them to live their lives more comfortably and confidently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with period cramps because they contain the root of the Chinese angelica plant, which has been used for centuries to alleviate menstrual discomfort. The capsules work by stimulating blood flow and reducing inflammation in the uterine lining, helping to ease cramping and spasms. Additionally, dong quai's natural estrogen-balancing properties can help regulate hormonal fluctuations that contribute to cramps. By promoting relaxation and soothing the muscles, dong quai capsules provide a natural and effective way to alleviate period pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with menstrual cramps by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antispasmodic properties: The bioactive compounds in Dong Quai, including apiol and ligustilide, can relax uterine muscles and alleviate spasms, helping to reduce the severity and duration of menstrual cramps.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong Quai's vasodilatory effects help to improve blood circulation, which may help to ease period pain by increasing the flow of oxygenated blood to the uterus and reducing the buildup of lactic acid, a major contributor to menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dong quai plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.
Barks: The barks of dong quai contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving period pain and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality angelica sinensis roots dried in dark glass containers for at least two years old.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1000mg of powdered angelica sinensis with 500mg of vegetable capsule shells and fill 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and moisture at room temperature.
Consume one capsule twice daily for three days before menstruation to alleviate symptoms of period cramps.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules help with period cramps because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and subsequent pain during menstruation. By blocking these contractions, ginger capsules can help alleviate cramping, bloating, and discomfort associated with PMS, allowing women to manage their symptoms more effectively and enjoy a greater sense of relief and comfort during this time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Vasodilation: The active compounds in ginger capsules, such as gingerol and paradol, can cause blood vessels to dilate, improving circulation and reducing blood flow to the uterus, which helps relieve cramp symptoms.

Pain-relieving properties: Ginger has been shown to have natural analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, which help reduce pain perception and alleviate the discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramp pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant contain salicylates, which have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, helping to ease cramping and discomfort during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginger roots of high quality with no added preservatives or chemicals weighing approximately 250 grams.
Grind the harvested dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix one part of the ground ginger powder with two parts of organic capsule filler such as potato starch or silica for uniform blending.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture in 250-500 milligram increments allowing for easy dosing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or until expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with period cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce prostaglandins in the body. Prostaglandins are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and subsequent cramping during menstruation. The active compound curcumin in turmeric capsules blocks the production of these prostaglandins, thus alleviating cramp severity and frequency. Additionally, turmeric's natural analgesic properties also help to soothe and calm the muscles, providing relief from painful menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory effects: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to menstrual cramps.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities: Turmeric's antioxidants help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to period cramp severity, while also reducing damage caused by free radicals that can exacerbate pain.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the production of pain-related chemicals in the brain, such as substance P and bradykinin, helping to reduce the sensation of menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The main storage organ of the plant, rhyzomes are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Roots: Turmeric roots are a concentrated source of bioactive compounds like turmerin, which has been shown to reduce prostaglandins and alleviate cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried turmeric root powder from the curcuma longa plant.
Weigh and mix 1000 milligrams of powdered ginger root into the turmeric mixture.
Combine the turmeric-ginger blend with 500 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for exactly 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate oil extraction.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the extracted curcumin-rich turmeric oil and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with period cramps because they contain salicin, a natural pain-relieving compound similar to aspirin. This active ingredient works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormones that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping during menstruation. By reducing these contractions, white willow capsules can help alleviate the severity and frequency of period cramps, allowing women to experience relief from discomfort and pain associated with their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin that helps reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which cause uterine contractions and cramping.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory compounds that help decrease swelling and pain in the uterus, pelvis, and abdomen, providing relief from cramps and discomfort associated with menstruation.

Pain-relieving compounds: White willow capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help relax muscles, reduce spasms, and ease menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with period cramps.
Barks: The barks of the white willow plant contain salicin and other bioactive compounds that help to alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba bark in early spring or late summer for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested bark using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the ground salix alba bark powder with 500mg of gelatin and 50ml of distilled water to form a uniform blend.
Fill clean, sterilized capsules with the herbal blend and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with period cramps because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that relaxes the uterine muscles and reduces inflammation. This helps to ease menstrual cramp discomfort by reducing the frequency and severity of contractions. Additionally, valerian has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause cramping. By taking valerian capsules, women may experience a significant reduction in period pain, allowing them to feel more comfortable and relaxed during their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Sedative and analgesic effects: Valerian root contains compounds that relax muscles, reduce pain perception, and induce a sense of calmness, helping to alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agents help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and subsequent pain during menstruation.

Soothing and calming effects on the nervous system: Valerian's GABA-enhancing properties can help regulate the body's response to stress and anxiety, which are often linked to worsened menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect on the uterus, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving period cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 20g of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 10:1 ratio of valeriana officinalis powder with 500mg gelatin capsules and fill each capsule individually.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose capsules helps with period cramps because of its unique blend of natural ingredients, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of guelder rose berries, which have been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual discomfort. Additionally, the presence of antioxidants in the formula helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cramping symptoms, providing relief from abdominal pain, bloating, and other associated symptoms. As a result, herbal guelder rose capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing period cramps.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Guelder Rose capsules have been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help relieve menstrual cramps by increasing blood flow and reducing spasms in the uterine arteries.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Guelder Rose contains various flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce prostaglandins (hormone-like substances) responsible for period pain.
Antispasmodic effects: The herbal extract has been found to relax smooth muscle contractions, including those in the uterus, helping to alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycosides and saponins, the roots of the Guelder Rose plant help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Barks: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, the barks of the plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried viburnum opulus leaves and stems in 50-gram quantities for processing.
Dry the harvested materials in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24-48 hours.
Grind the dried viburnum opulus into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of viburnum opulus powder with 200 milliliters of pure water in a glass jar to create a liquid extract for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the viburnum opulus liquid extract and seal securely for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with period cramps because they contain the extract of Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate menstrual discomfort. The herb's flavonoids and alkaloids work together to relax uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity and frequency. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions during menstruation. By targeting these underlying mechanisms, maypop capsules provide natural relief from period cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms and feel more comfortable during their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Maypop capsules contain passionflower alkaloids, which have been shown to effectively reduce menstrual cramp pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in maypop capsules have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax uterine muscles and reduce inflammation that can contribute to period cramps.

Folate: Maypop capsules are a natural source of folate, a B vitamin that plays a crucial role in hormone regulation and has been shown to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps by reducing prostaglandin production, which causes uterine contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Maypop contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce prostaglandins, which cause cramping and discomfort during menstruation.
Barks: The bark of Maypop contains flavonoids and terpenes that have a sedative effect on the uterine muscles, helping to relax and ease menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passiflora incarnata flowers in amounts of 30g for later use.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herb with 100mg of vegetable cellulose capsules in a ratio of 1:1 for 10 minutes.
Fill each capsule with approximately 50mg of the herbal mixture and seal tightly within 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with period cramps because they contain isoflavones, which have a natural ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce inflammation. The isoflavones in red clover also mimic the body's own estrogen, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to menstrual cramp severity. Additionally, red clover has been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and amplify period pain. By addressing these underlying causes, red clover capsules provide effective relief from debilitating period cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to help alleviate menstrual cramps by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions.
Antioxidants: Red clover's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to the alleviation of period pain and discomfort.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens, such as genistein and daidzein, have been found to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing period pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried red clover flowers and sift through a fine-mesh strainer to remove any debris.
Combine the sifted flowers with 200ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container to extract the liquid essence.
Mix 5g of the extracted liquid essence with 95g of gelatin powder and add 50ml of glycerin to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared filling and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue cohosh capsules helps with period cramps because they contain compounds that have a natural ability to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. The herbal remedy also has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, blue cohosh contains flavonoids and saponins which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce prostaglandin levels, contributing to a reduction in period cramp severity. By addressing the underlying physiological processes, blue cohosh capsules can provide natural relief from debilitating menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic activity: Blue cohosh contains compounds that help to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The herbs in blue cohosh have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and pain during menstruation.

Vasodilatory action: Blue cohosh's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve blood flow to the uterus, reducing menstrual cramp severity by increasing oxygen delivery and nutrient supply to the uterine muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relieve menstrual cramp pain.
Rhizomes: Rich in alkaloids, particularly caudatine, which has been shown to effectively reduce uterine contractions and alleviate period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh caulophyllum thalictroides roots in early spring or fall when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested roots using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by air drying for several weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve a consistent texture.
Mix one part of the powdered caulophyllum thalictroides with one part of vegetable glycerin in a small bowl, stirring until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using approximately 250-500mg per capsule, and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blazing Star Capsules helps with period cramps because they contain a unique blend of herbs, including Lamiaceae species, that work to alleviate muscle contractions in the uterus. The capsules also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine cramping. Additionally, the herbal formula relaxes smooth muscles and calms nervous tension, further reducing menstrual discomfort. By addressing the root causes of period cramps, Blazing Star Capsules provide effective relief from crampy abdominal pain, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Cramp Bark (Viburnum opulus): Contains scopoletin, a natural compound that helps to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with period cramps.
Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Contains curcumin, a polyphenol that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce menstrual cramp severity and frequency.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like curcuminoids and sesquiterpenes, rhyzomes exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried chamaelirium luteum root bark from a reputable source.
Grind the dried root bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground root bark with 50% fillers such as potato starch or silica gel in a 1:1 ratio by weight for 2 hours.
Fill empty #0 capsules with a mixture of 250-300 milligrams of the herbal blend per capsule and seal immediately for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and humidity for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with period cramps because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation in the body. The capsules contain a compound called beta-sitosterol, which has been shown to block the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to painful menstrual cramps. By reducing prostaglandin production, saw palmetto capsules can help alleviate cramping, providing relief from discomfort during menstruation. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to ease period pain and is a popular alternative to traditional medications.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's ability to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) helps reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, thus alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which may exacerbate period cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Prostaglandin inhibition: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and contribute to menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant have been traditionally used to ease menstrual discomfort and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for making medicinal capsules for period cramps.
Fruits: Saw palmetto fruits contain fatty acids and other compounds that help relax uterine muscles, reducing cramping and pain associated with menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried serenoa repens leaves from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered serenoa repens with 500 milligrams of vitamin b6 and 200 milliliters of organic coconut oil to create a uniform blend for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal them tightly to preserve potency for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature and consume three times daily as needed for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose capsules helps with period cramps because they contain a potent natural ingredient called gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). GLA has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. By reducing prostaglandins, evening primrose capsules can alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms, providing relief from discomfort, pain, and swelling associated with PMS. Additionally, GLA helps to regulate hormonal balance, further contributing to a reduction in period cramps.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Fatty Acids: Evening primrose oil is rich in essential fatty acids, particularly omega-6 fatty acids, which can help reduce prostaglandins that cause uterine contractions and cramping.

Vitamins and Minerals: Evening primrose capsules contain a range of vitamins (e.g., B1, B2, C, E) and minerals (e.g., potassium, magnesium), which can help alleviate menstrual symptoms by regulating hormonal imbalances, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the evening primrose plant contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a fatty acid that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate cramp symptoms.
Seeds: Evening primrose seeds are rich in GLA, which has been shown to relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest oenothera biennis roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned oenothera biennis roots for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried oenothera biennis roots into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 250mg of the ground oenothera biennis root powder with other herbs as needed.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal blend and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peruvian balsam capsules helps with period cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Myroxylon pereirae, contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms in the uterus, thereby alleviating cramping symptoms. Additionally, Peruvian balsam has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm the body and mind during menstruation, further reducing the severity of cramps. As a result, taking Peruvian balsam capsules may provide effective relief from period cramp discomfort.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Peruvian Balsam contains a combination of volatile oils, including cinnamic acid and vanillin, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation, providing relief from menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic properties: The capsules' active ingredients, such as alpha-pinene and beta-caryophyllene, have been found to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that cause period cramps.

Oxytocin receptor antagonism: Peruvian Balsam's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to block oxytocin receptors in the uterus, which helps reduce uterine contraction and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Peruvian balsam contain a high concentration of resin, which has been traditionally used to reduce menstrual cramps and pain.
Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help to alleviate menstrual discomfort and reduce period cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Gather one hundred grams of dried myroxylon balsamum resin and fifty grams of powdered ginger root.
Grind the myroxylon balsamum resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for ten minutes.
Combine the ground myroxylon balsamum powder with the powdered ginger root in a large mixing bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one half teaspoon of the myroxylon balsamum-ginger blend and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with period cramps because they contain a powerful herb called Paeonia lactiflora, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to alleviate menstrual discomfort. The herb's active compounds, including paeoniflorin and benzoylmethane, work together to reduce inflammation and relax the uterine muscles, thereby decreasing the frequency and intensity of period cramps. This natural remedy also helps to regulate hormonal imbalances and improve overall uterine health, making it a popular choice for women seeking relief from menstrual pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peony's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help reduce inflammation and alleviate cramp pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause uterine contractions.

Stabilizing effect on the menstrual cycle: Chinese Peony has a stabilizing effect on the uterine lining, preventing excessive shedding and reducing the severity of cramps caused by hormonal imbalances or irregular menstruation.
Pain-relieving property: The capsules' analgesic and sedative compounds, such as paeoniflorin and benzaldehyde, help relieve cramp pain by blocking pain signals to the brain and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate menstrual pain and cramps by reducing prostaglandins.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Chinese Peony have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help relax uterine muscles and reduce cramping during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried paeonia lactiflora roots in sufficient quantity for your desired yield of capsules.
Measure out one part of dried paeonia lactiflora root and mix it with one part of excipient like silica gel or potato starch.
Grind the mixed herb and excipient into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Sift the powdered mixture through a #60 mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 250-300mg of the sifted herbal powder and seal immediately, storing them in an airtight container for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with period cramps because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, it can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and ease menstrual cramp pain. Additionally, licorice root extract is known to relax the uterine muscles, further alleviating discomfort and reducing spasms. As a natural remedy, herbal licorice capsules provide a safe and effective way to alleviate period cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms and maintain a comfortable lifestyle during their periods.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramp discomfort.

Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce prostaglandin production, a hormone-like substance that causes uterine contractions leading to cramps.
Isoflavones: These compounds have a mild estrogenic effect, which can help to balance hormonal fluctuations during menstruation and reduce symptoms of PMS, including cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a significant amount of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective for relieving period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dry glycyrrhiza glabra roots in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage and preparation time of 2 weeks.
Grind the harvested dried roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours.
Mix the powdered root with 200mg of magnesium stearate as a lubricant and store it in an airtight container for 1 day.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture, aiming for a total weight of 500mg per capsule and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine within 3 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation and use as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with period cramps because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, feverfew capsules can reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. Additionally, the herb's ability to relax smooth muscle tissues can help alleviate cramp severity and frequency. As a result, many women report experiencing less severe and more manageable menstrual cramps when supplementing with feverfew capsules during their periods.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's parthenolide content helps to relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity and duration by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause uterine contractions.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to period pain, bloating, and discomfort.

Antispasmodic effects: Feverfew's antispasmodic properties help to ease muscle spasms, cramps, and contractions associated with menstruation by relaxing smooth muscles and improving blood flow, thereby reducing period pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the feverfew plant contain a high concentration of parthenolide, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve menstrual cramp symptoms.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers are used in medicinal capsules due to their ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers into a mortar and pestle for grinding.
Add 10 grams of powdered ginger root to the tanacetum parthenium mixture for enhanced absorption.
Grind the herbal blend in the mortar and pestle with 50 milliliters of vodka as a solvent for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid extract through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 1 hour.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the tanacetum parthenium-ginger extract and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with period cramps because they contain mucilages, which soothe and lubricate the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax the uterine muscles, alleviating cramping sensations. Additionally, marshmallow's antioxidant-rich compounds may help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to painful contractions during menstruation. By targeting these underlying causes of period pain, herbal marshmallow capsules provide a natural and effective solution for managing menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with menstrual cramps.
Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow root can relax the uterine muscles and relieve cramping and spasms caused by menstruation.

Soothing demulcent properties: Marshmallow root's mucilage can help protect and soothe the digestive tract, which can be irritated during menstruation, reducing symptoms like bloating, gas, and cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
Seeds: The seeds contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe digestive issues and ease cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis weighing 100g for maximum potency.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle taking approximately 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of ground marshmallow root with 80g of finely powdered vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend requiring about 5 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal blend ensuring each capsule contains exactly 500mg for optimal dosing using a precision scoop and timer set for approximately 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerate for up to 2 years to preserve potency and freshness taking less than 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with period cramps because its active ingredient, hyperforin, has a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect. This allows it to directly target the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and relieving discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and contribute to cramping. As a result, taking St John's Wort capsules can provide effective relief from period cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: St. John's Wort capsules contain flavonoids that can help relax muscle spasms and reduce menstrual cramp severity by blocking the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The herb contains hyperforin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, including uterine inflammation, which may contribute to period cramps and other symptoms.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: St. John's Wort capsules contain various flavonoid compounds that have been found to exhibit analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, potentially helping to reduce menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, which have been shown to be effective in reducing menstrual cramp severity.
Flowers: The flowers contain a range of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate period pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry st john's wort flowers ensuring optimal potency for future use.
Grind dried st john's wort flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a carrier such as silica gel in a 4:1 ratio to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture weighing approximately 250 milligrams per capsule.
Store the capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or refrigerate for extended shelf life.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat period cramps with herbal capsules is to combine a blend of natural herbs that have anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. Ginger, turmeric, and chamomile are popular options that help reduce pain and discomfort. Additionally, capsules containing willow bark and black cohosh can also provide relief by reducing inflammation and soothing the uterine muscles.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to period cramps that are treated with herbal capsules are endometriosis, menorrhagia, and dysmenorrhea. Herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and willow bark have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms like pelvic pain, bloating, and discomfort associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbs like chamomile and passionflower can help soothe anxiety and stress related to menstrual issues.
